en.extract:
The medium tree finch (Camarhynchus pauper) is a species of Darwin's finch. It is endemic to the Galápagos Islands, where it occurs only on Floreana Island. Its name derives from its intermediate size between the small tree finch and large tree finch. Due to its limited geographic range and its high breeding mortality following the introduction of the parasitic avian vampire fly, the medium tree finch is listed as critically endangered on the IUCN Red List.


Distribution
This species is only found on Floreana Island at elevations above 250 meters (820 ft) in moist highland forest habitats, across an range of about 24 km². Prime breeding habitat is dominated by Scalesia pedunculata trees, although the species also occurs in the surrounding agricultural zones. A 2008 survey estimated the number of medium tree finches as 1,620, while a series of surveys between 2014 and 2016 put the number of medium tree finch territories at between 3900 and 4700.


Ecology

The diurnal Galapagos short-eared owl is its only remaining natural predator. Medium tree finches generally lay two to three eggs. Eggs are incubated for approximately 12 days, and nestlings are fed by both parents at the nest for approximately 14 days before becoming fledglings. The beak of the medium tree finch is intermediate in size between the small tree finch and large tree finch.


Status
The medium tree finch is threatened by introduced predators such as rats, mice, cats, and the smooth-billed ani, as well as habitat loss, which has occurred through clearance for agriculture.
The introduced avian vampire fly (Philornis downsi) is a significant threat to the survival of this species. The fly's parasitic larvae reside inside the nest material and feed on the blood and body tissues of nestlings. This parasitism causes high nestling mortality in the medium tree finch. Because of this, and because it has a very small range on a single island, the medium tree finch is listed as critically endangered on the IUCN Red List.


References


External links

BirdLife Species Factsheet.

The '''medium tree finch''' ('''''Camarhynchus pauper''''') is a species of [[Darwin's finches|Darwin's finch]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to the [[Galápagos Islands]], where it occurs only on [[Floreana Island]]. Its name derives from its intermediate size between the [[small tree finch]] and [[large tree finch]]. Due to its limited geographic range and its high breeding mortality following the introduction of the parasitic [[avian vampire fly]], the medium tree finch is listed as [[critically endangered]] on the [[IUCN Red List]].<ref name=iucn/>

==Distribution==
This species is only found on [[Floreana Island]] at elevations above {{Convert|250|m|sp=us}} in moist highland forest habitats, across an range of about 24 km².<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last=Dvorak |first=Michael |last2=Nemeth |first2=Erwin |last3=Wendelin |first3=Beate |last4=Herrera |first4=Patricio |last5=Mosquera |first5=Denis |last6=Anchundia |first6=David |last7=Sevilla |first7=Christian |last8=Tebbich |first8=Sabine |last9=Fessl |first9=Birgit |date=2017-05-11 |title=Conservation status of landbirds on Floreana: the smallest inhabited Galápagos Island |url=https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/jofo.12197 |journal=Journal of Field Ornithology |language=en |volume=88 |issue=2 |pages=132–145 |doi=10.1111/jofo.12197|url-access=subscription }}</ref> Prime breeding habitat is dominated by ''[[Scalesia pedunculata]]'' trees, although the species also occurs in the surrounding agricultural zones.<ref name=":0" /> A 2008 survey estimated the number of medium tree finches as 1,620,<ref>{{Cite journal |last=O’Connor |first=Jody A. |last2=Sulloway |first2=Frank J. |last3=Kleindorfer |first3=Sonia |date=2010-04-06 |title=Avian population survey in the Floreana highlands: is Darwin’s Medium Tree Finch declining in remnant patches of Scalesia forest? |url=https://www.cambridge.org/core/product/identifier/S0959270910000195/type/journal_article |journal=Bird Conservation International |language=en |volume=20 |issue=4 |pages=343–353 |doi=10.1017/S0959270910000195 |issn=0959-2709}}</ref> while a series of surveys between 2014 and 2016 put the number of medium tree finch territories at between 3900 and 4700.<ref name=":0" />

==Ecology==
[[File:Young male medium tree finch on Floreana Island in the Galápagos Islands.jpg|left|thumb|Young male medium tree finch on [[Floreana Island]]]]
The diurnal [[Short-eared owl|Galapagos short-eared owl]] is its only remaining natural predator. Medium tree finches generally lay two to three eggs. Eggs are incubated for approximately 12 days, and nestlings are fed by both parents at the nest for approximately 14 days before becoming fledglings. The beak of the medium tree finch is intermediate in size between the [[small tree finch]] and [[large tree finch]].

==Status==
The medium tree finch is threatened by introduced predators such as rats, mice, cats, and the [[smooth-billed ani]], as well as [[habitat loss]], which has occurred through clearance for agriculture.

The introduced [[avian vampire fly]] (''Philornis downsi'') is a significant threat to the survival of this species. The fly's parasitic larvae reside inside the nest material and feed on the blood and body tissues of nestlings. This parasitism causes high nestling mortality in the medium tree finch.<ref>{{Cite journal |last=O’Connor |first=Jody A. |last2=Sulloway |first2=Frank J. |last3=Robertson |first3=Jeremy |last4=Kleindorfer |first4=Sonia |date=2009-11-11 |title=''Philornis downsi'' parasitism is the primary cause of nestling mortality in the critically endangered Darwin’s medium tree finch (''Camarhynchus pauper'') |url=http://link.springer.com/10.1007/s10531-009-9740-1 |journal=Biodiversity and Conservation |language=en |volume=19 |issue=3 |pages=853–866 |doi=10.1007/s10531-009-9740-1 |issn=0960-3115|url-access=subscription }}</ref> Because of this, and because it has a very small range on a single island, the medium tree finch is listed as critically endangered on the [[IUCN Red List]].<ref name="iucn" />

zh.extract:
中树雀（台湾作查理樹雀，学名：Camarhynchus pauper），是雀形目唐纳雀科的一种鸟类。
中树雀体重约16克，翼长约69毫米，嘴峰长约14.6毫米，喙宽度约5.2毫米，喙厚度约8.3毫米，跗蹠长约22.1毫米，尾长约48.9毫米。中树雀在其分布区内为留鸟，其栖息在密集的高大树木组成的森林中，食性为肉食性，主要食物来源是陆生无脊椎动物。
中树雀分布于加拉帕戈斯群岛弗雷里安納島的湿润灌丛。该物种是单型物种，没有亚种分化。
